title: omphalodes verna subsp. graeca
intro: Omphalodes verna subsp. graeca, also known as Greek navelwort, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the forget-me-not family (Boraginaceae). Traditionally, the plant has been used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can be used to treat wounds and skin conditions. The health benefits of Omphalodes verna subsp. graeca include its ability to soothe and calm irritated skin, reduce inflammation, and promote the healing process. In herbal preparations, the plant is typically dried and made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to treat various health issues, although further research is needed to confirm its effectiveness.
